error.signature: nginx: [alert] socket() failed (24: Too many open files)

verdict.summary: nginx process hit the OS file descriptor limit. Each connection uses at least 2 FDs (client + upstream). Proxy doubles this.

dead_ends.action: Restart nginx to temporarily fix the issue
dead_ends.why_fails: Restarting reclaims FDs but the limit is hit again under the same load. Must increase the limit.

dead_ends.action: Only set ulimit in the shell before starting nginx
dead_ends.why_fails: Shell ulimit does not persist across service restarts. Must configure in systemd unit or nginx.conf.

workarounds.how: worker_rlimit_nofile 65536;  # in main context of nginx.conf; sets RLIMIT_NOFILE for worker processes

workarounds.action: Increase limits in systemd service unit
workarounds.how: In /etc/systemd/system/nginx.service.d/override.conf: [Service] LimitNOFILE=65536; then: systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart nginx

* soft nofile 65536
* hard nofile 65536
# Also check: sysctl fs.file-max
